name: Micafungin
executiveSummary: IV echinocandin for candidiasis and prophylaxis in transplant patients; one daily dose.
indications:
## Primary indications

- Esophageal candidiasis and candidemia.
- Candidiasis prophylaxis in transplant and high-risk ICU patients.

## Precautions

- Adjust in severe hepatic impairment. Hemolysis (rare).

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Lyophilized powder vial containing 50 mg or 100 mg (Mycamine).
adulto.reconstitution: 5 mL of NS. Conc: 10 mg/mL (50 mg) or 20 mg/mL (100 mg). Gently swirl; do not shake vigorously.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 100 mL of NS and administer over 60 min. Protect from light.
adulto.notes: Do not shake vigorously (generates foam). Protect from light throughout preparation and infusion. Do not use D5W for reconstitution. Usual dose for invasive candidiasis: 100 mg/day; prophylaxis in transplant: 50 mg/day. Echinocandin with no dose adjustment required in renal impairment or mild-to-moderate hepatic impairment.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 24 h at room temperature, protected from light.

## Diluted solution (to be administered)

- 24 h at room temperature, protected from light.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Phlebitis, elevation of alkaline phosphatase, rash, nausea.

bibliography.citation: Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.
Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA). Clinical guidelines.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
